title: Nervilia gammieana
taxonomy: Nervilia gammieana is classified as a species of orchid in the genus Nervilia and family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the subtribe Nerviliinae and tribe Coelinae. The plant is also known as the gammie's nerite or gammy's nerite. Nervilia gammieana is a type of terrestrial orchid native to Sri Lanka. The classification of Nervilia gammieana reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the orchid family.

intro: Nervilia gammieana, also known as Gammie's butterfly orchid, is a species of orchid native to Sri Lanka, characterized by its unique, heart-shaped leaves and delicate, white flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which can aid in soothing skin irritations and wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Nervilia gammieana include flavonoids, glycosides, and phenolic acids, which are thought to contribute to its therapeutic effects.
